summary: On 15 May Ukraine’s air defenses shot down a massive wave of Russian drones and missiles, reporting 130 Shahed‑type UAVs and one anti‑ship missile destroyed. Simultaneously, Russia struck the Ryazan oil refinery, a key fuel hub for Moscow, while Ukraine completed the first phase of a 1,000‑on‑1,000 prisoner exchange, bringing home 205 servicemen. Drone activity continues across multiple fronts, with notable incursions over Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and the Black Sea corridor.
key_points: Massive drone swarm intercepted (130 UAVs) on 15 May (kpszsu/61910)
Ryazan refinery hit, threatening Moscow’s fuel supply (UkraineNow/81203)
First 1,000‑on‑1,000 POW exchange delivers 205 Ukrainian soldiers home (DeepStateUA/23506)
Continued Shahed attacks on Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Black Sea routes (kpszsu/61903, kpszsu/61904, kpszsu/61906)
Alert of possible ballistic missile use from the south (kpszsu/61936)
Local civilian incident: mayor of Lviv doused with dirty liquid (ukr_leaks_eng/30190)
Ukrainian authorities increase alert groups in Kyiv by 40% (UkraineNow/81233)

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine’s air defense neutralised a large-scale Russian UAV and missile barrage on 15 May, downing 130 Shahed‑type drones and one anti‑ship missile (kpszsu/61910). Russia simultaneously struck the Ryazan oil refinery, a critical fuel source for the capital, underscoring its strategic targeting of logistics (UkraineNow/81203). Ukraine completed the first phase of a 1,000‑on‑1,000 prisoner swap, repatriating 205 servicemen, marking a significant humanitarian development (DeepStateUA/23506).

points: Air defenses shot down 130 hostile UAVs and one anti‑ship missile in a coordinated strike (kpszsu/61910).
Kyiv increased civil alert groups by 40% to bolster mobilisation readiness (UkraineNow/81233).
First phase of the 1,000‑on‑1,000 exchange returned 205 Ukrainian POWs, many former Mariupol defenders (DeepStateUA/23506).
Continued reports of Shahed attacks on multiple cities, including Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and the Black Sea corridor (kpszsu/61903, kpszsu/61904, kpszsu/61906).
Local security incident in Lviv where the mayor was doused with an unknown liquid (ukr_leaks_eng/30190).
Deployed five X‑31P anti‑radar missiles and a X‑35 anti‑ship missile from the Crimean airbase in the 15 May attack (kpszsu/61910).
Struck the Ryazan refinery, a key source of gasoline and aviation fuel for Moscow (UkraineNow/81203).
Launched a large drone swarm from the Black Sea and other directions, indicating expanded UAV launch sites (kpszsu/61910).
Issued a warning of possible ballistic missile use from the south (kpszsu/61936).
Reported 355 drones shot down across Russia during the night of 14‑15 May (UkraineNow/81203).
Shahed UAVs reported over Kharkiv from the north and east (kpszsu/61903, kpszsu/61904).
Group of UAVs targeted Kryvyi Rih from the south (kpszsu/61906).
Multiple Shahed sightings in Chernihiv and Poltava regions (eRadarrua/91896, eRadarrua/91870).
Black Sea‑origin UAVs heading toward Odesa/Mykolaiv area (kpszsu/61906).
Continued radar alerts for Shahed incursions near Boryspil and Brovary (mon1tor_ua/66732, mon1tor_ua/66742).
UAVs detected over Kyiv and Vyshorod from the north (kpszsu/61904, kpszsu/61929).
Shahed activity reported over Brovary and Borodyanka (mon1tor_ua/66732, mon1tor_ua/66740).
Air raid alert sounded in Kyiv at 11:39 on 15 May (eRadarrua/91857).
No‑kill zones reported as “clean” over Kyiv outskirts (mon1tor_ua/66744).
X‑31P missile launch trajectories included northern Russian regions (kpszsu/61910).
UAVs from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, Mykolaiv, and the southern coast (kpszsu/61906, kpszsu/61912).
25 Shahed drones launched from the Black Sea towards Pivdenne/Rybakivka (mon1tor_ua/66729).
Alert issued for Odessa as a hostile UAV approached the city (kpszsu/61922).
Group of UAVs observed over Nikopol from the east (kpszsu/61906).
Potential ballistic missile threat warned from the south (kpszsu/61936).
Ukrainian forces intercepted 130 Shahed‑type UAVs and one X‑35 anti‑ship missile on 15 May (kpszsu/61910).
Five X‑31P anti‑radar missiles failed to reach targets, indicating effective air defence (kpszsu/61910).
Russian air defence claimed 355 drone kills across Russia during the same night (UkraineNow/81203).
Continued low‑altitude UAV activity over multiple oblasts, requiring sustained radar monitoring (mon1tor_ua posts).
Ukrainian air assets engaged in intercepts across north, south, and east sectors (kpszsu/61910).

key_developments: Ukraine’s air defence downed 130 Shahed‑type UAVs and one anti‑ship missile in a coordinated 15 May attack (Source: @kpszsu/61910).
Russia struck the Ryazan oil refinery, threatening fuel supplies for Moscow (Source: @UkraineNow/81203).
First phase of the 1,000‑on‑1,000 prisoner exchange returned 205 Ukrainian servicemen home (Source: @DeepStateUA/23506).
Black Sea‑origin UAVs continue to target Odesa and Mykolaiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/61906).
Ukraine increased Kyiv alert groups by 40% to strengthen mobilisation (Source: @UkraineNow/81233).
Russia warned of possible ballistic missile use from the south (Source: @kpszsu/61936).
Multiple Shahed sightings reported across Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, indicating dispersed launch sites (Source: @kpszsu/61903).
sections.threat_assessment: The high‑volume UAV and missile barrage demonstrates Russia’s reliance on saturation attacks to overwhelm Ukrainian air defences, while the Ryazan refinery strike signals a strategic effort to disrupt Russian logistics. Ukraine’s robust interception record shows improving defensive capability, yet the persistent southern Black Sea UAV corridor and the hinted ballistic missile threat keep pressure on critical infrastructure. Continued drone activity across all fronts will likely force Ukraine to allocate more air‑defence assets, potentially stretching resources.
sections.regional_response: Western partners have praised Ukraine’s defensive performance, and the successful POW exchange reflects diplomatic momentum facilitated by the United States. Russia’s targeting of energy assets may prompt further sanctions or diplomatic pressure, especially from EU states concerned about regional fuel security. Neighboring NATO members remain vigilant, monitoring spill‑over risks from increased aerial activity.
sections.intelligence_notes: The concentration of Shahed UAVs from multiple vectors suggests expanded launch pads in occupied territories, including the Black Sea coast and eastern oblasts. The failure of X‑31P missiles to achieve objectives indicates effective Ukrainian electronic‑war and missile‑defence measures. Monitoring of ballistic missile launch preparations in the south is advised, as the threat warning may precede a limited use of longer‑range systems.
